{
  "communicative_function": "Asserting radical self-agency and the necessity of internal initiative for change",
  "implicit_claims": ["Only the self possesses the capacity to modify its own condition", "No external person or force can effect such alteration", "Personal condition is fundamentally self-determined"],
  "coherence_assessment": "Coherent within a framework of radical individualism; tension arises only if 'condition' includes unalterable external facts",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "Contexts that prioritize motivational self-reliance and treat circumstances as responsive to internal volition",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Situations where structural, medical, or interpersonal factors demonstrably constrain or enable change beyond individual control"
